sprint.name: Two starter tickets generated by `koru --init`. Replace them with

STARTER-001.description:
Verify the koru ↔ planfile ↔ LLM loop end-to-end on this project.

1. Run `koru` (no args) and read the markdown brief.
2. Confirm the policy table shows all gates as `False`.
3. Confirm `STARTER-001` is the active ticket.
4. Run `planfile ticket done STARTER-001`.
STARTER-001.name: Confirm koru is wired correctly

STARTER-002.description:
Edit `.planfile/sprints/current.yaml` and add the work that
actually matters for this project. You can also re-run
`koru --init --force --from your-pipeline.yaml` to import a
flat pipeline you already maintain elsewhere.
STARTER-002.name: Replace starter tickets with your real backlog

STARTER-019.description:
[OPERATOR] Kalibracja czatu OS injectora (auto)

brak kalibracji chatu dla auto — task koru:ide-os:calibrate IDE=auto

Wykonaj w IDE, potem zamknij ticket: `task tickets:done -- <id>` lub planfile ticket done.

Weryfikacja (opcjonalnie): `task koru:ide-os:calibrate IDE=auto`

history.message:
[OPERATOR] Kalibracja czatu OS injectora (auto)

brak kalibracji chatu dla auto — task koru:ide-os:calibrate IDE=auto

Wykonaj w IDE, potem zamknij ticket: `task tickets:done -- <id>` lub planfile ticket done.

Weryfikacja (opcjonalnie): `task koru:ide-os:calibrate IDE=auto`

inputs.prompt: HUMAN-ONLY GUI step, cannot be done headless. (1) Focus the running Qoder window and open its AI chat panel. (2) Hover the mouse pointer exactly over the chat text-input field and HOLD STILL. (3) Run: koru autopilot calibrate --ide qoder  — it captures the live pointer position after a 5s delay and writes the qoder entry to ~/.koru/ide-os-injector.json, so the pointer MUST be on the chat field or the entry is bogus. (4) Verify: koru autopilot route --ide qoder  — the vdisplay_verified_injection line (currently: no chat calibration for qoder) must flip from X to OK. Only that one line is in scope; the typing/injection routes stay X on this GNOME/Wayland session (needs KORU_ALLOW_BLIND_KEYBOARD_FALLBACK=1 or the qoder IDE plugin) — separate follow-on, do not block on it.

STARTER-019.name: [OPERATOR] Kalibracja czatu OS injectora (auto) brak kalibracji chatu dla auto — task koru:ide-os:calibrate IDE=auto ...

notes: Verified read-only: 'task' CLI is not present in this env — real tool is 'koru'/'planfile' (task koru:ide-os:calibrate IDE=auto == koru autopilot calibrate --ide auto). 'koru autopilot route --ide auto' resolves auto->qoder and reports calibration=False: 'no chat calibration for qoder in ide-os-injector.json'. Existing profile ~/.koru/ide-os-injector.json has cursor/jetbrains/main/vscode/windsurf but NO qoder entry — this is the exact gap. calibrate is interactive mouse-capture (--delay-seconds, default 5) requiring a human to hover the qoder chat input; running it headless would overwrite the valid profile with garbage coords, so it was NOT auto-run. Also flagged: on this Wayland session all injection backends are currently non-viable (xdotool needs x11; wtype unsupported; ydotool guarded=focus window cannot be identified / blind needs KORU_ALLOW_BLIND_KEYBOARD_FALLBACK=1; no qoder plugin), so calibration alone will not yet yield a viable control route.

Verified 2026-07-05: 'qoder' absent from ~/.koru/ide-os-injector.json (only cursor/jetbrains/main/vscode/windsurf present). 'route --ide qoder' fails on 'no chat calibration for qoder'. Qoder IS running (pid=46098). Backends ydotool+wl-copy present; route remains non-viable on Wayland without blind-fallback opt-in or qoder plugin. Did NOT run calibrate headless — it captures live pointer coords and would write a bogus entry. Correctly left in waiting_input per executor=human/interactive.

Re-verified 2026-07-06: state unchanged from 2026-07-05. Qoder pid 46098 alive (/home/tom/.qoder/shared_client/bin/0.19.5/x86_64_linux/Qoder start); a second /usr/share/qoder/qoder instance is open on /home/tom/github/semcod/koru. ~/.koru/ide-os-injector.json still has only cursor/jetbrains/main/vscode/windsurf — no qoder entry. koru autopilot route --ide qoder reports calibration=False (no chat calibration for qoder), plugin=False, blind_opt_in=False (KORU_ALLOW_BLIND_KEYBOARD_FALLBACK unset), session=wayland desktop=ubuntu:gnome; no viable control route, exit 1. Did NOT run calibrate headless — it needs a human hovering over the live chat input; a headless capture would write bogus coords. Nothing an agent can advance without the human GUI step; remains correctly blocked on executor=human/interactive.

Investigated 2026-07-06, cannot self-serve. Verified: pid 46098 alive (Qoder start, 14h20m). Session=wayland/gnome. usage: koru autopilot calibrate [-h] [--ide IDE] [--delay-seconds SECONDS]
                                [--config FILE] [--project DIR]
                                [--prompt TEXT]

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --ide IDE             Profile id (windsurf, vscode, vscodium, cursor, ...).
                        Default auto: same detection as `drive --direct --ide
                        auto` (focused IDE when known, else first running).
  --delay-seconds SECONDS
                        Seconds to wait before capturing the mouse position
                        (default: 5).
  --config FILE         Optional ide-os-injector.json path (default:
                        ~/.koru/ide-os-injector.json).
  --project DIR         Project root whose env2llm registry is refreshed after
                        calibration (default: cwd).
  --prompt TEXT         Optional smoke prompt injected immediately after
                        saving the profile. shows NO --x/--y coordinate flag — it strictly captures the live mouse position after a 5s delay (--delay-seconds, default 5), so a headless run would write a bogus qoder entry (the exact failure mode this ticket warns against). Programmatic pointer placement is non-viable here: route reports focus_detection=False and 'focused window cannot be identified on this compositor'; I have no reliable coordinates for the chat input field and cannot open/focus the chat panel headlessly. No qoder entry exists in ~/.koru/ide-os-injector.json yet (only cursor/jetbrains/main/vscode/windsurf). route --ide qoder confirms the target line is currently X: 'vdisplay_verified_injection ... no chat calibration for qoder'. Did NOT run calibrate to avoid corrupting the config with a bogus entry. Genuinely blocked on a human to position the physical pointer; not an implementation failure and not complete.

Agent verification 2026-07-06: Qoder IS alive — pid 46098 is /home/tom/.qoder/shared_client/bin/0.19.5/x86_64_linux/Qoder start plus many Electron children (VSCode-fork webview). Session is GNOME/Wayland (WAYLAND_DISPLAY=wayland-0, XDG_SESSION_TYPE=wayland, DISPLAY=:0 is XWayland only). ~/.koru/ide-os-injector.json has cursor/jetbrains/main/vscode/windsurf but NO qoder entry. Read-only koru autopilot route --ide qoder confirms qoder (calibration=False, plugin=False) and the X line: vdisplay_verified_injection = no chat calibration for qoder. Did NOT run calibrate: it captures the live pointer after a 5s delay and the pointer is currently at x:6816 y:6836 (multi-monitor, NOT on the chat field); running it headless would write a bogus qoder coordinate that route would then falsely mark OK, and downstream drive/injection would click+type at the wrong screen location (risk of typing into the wrong window, e.g. a terminal — actively harmful). xdotool and ydotool are installed, but Qoder is an Electron app on a multi-monitor Wayland+XWayland session and the chat input field absolute screen coords cannot be reliably discovered headless; fabricating them is not a valid calibration. Task remains genuinely human/interactive (executor.kind=human, mode=interactive) — needs a human at the mouse. All other typing/injection routes are also X on this session and are out of scope per the ticket.

STARTER-021.name: Repair VALLM validation findings

notes: Investigated. 11/13 errors were false positives from a STALE .venv missing pytest+toml (both correctly declared in pyproject.toml; Taskfile's own 'task install' -> pip install -e .[dev] was already correct, just never run for this venv). Fixed by running task install: full test suite now runs (239 passed, previously could not run pytest at all). Spot-verified via 'vallm validate -f tests/test_cli.py': now PASS/1.00 vs stale report's error. Remaining 1 error (sumd/rules.pl: tree-sitter found 21 parse errors in 'perl') is a vallm language-detection ambiguity -- .pl is Prolog here (sumd_logic_validator), not Perl -- not a sumd code bug. Report itself was stale (dated 2026-05-22, referenced old path ~/github/oqlos/sumd which no longer exists; project has since moved to semcod/sumd). Full batch re-scan (211 files) times out in this session (>90s); left for a dedicated run, not blocking since underlying code issues are resolved.

context.input: `project/validation.toon.yaml` reports 13 error(s) and 6 warning(s). Fix the highest-impact validation failures first, then re-run `vallm validate` / the project validation task and refresh the report.
